title: cuphea rhodocalyx
intro: Cuphea rhodocalyx, also known as firecracker plant, is a flowering perennial shrub native to Mexico and Central America. It is a member of the Lythraceae family and is valued for its vibrant red, orange, and yellow flowers, as well as its medicinal properties. The plant contains anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Cuphea rhodocalyx can be used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
